blocks.text: Based on the lighting switch input signal, the MICU controls relays to turn each light on and off. The combination meter turns on/off the high beam indicator and light ON indicator based on the B-CAN signal transmitted from the MICU.
Auto light on/off control (vehicles equipped with auto light system)

The position lights and headlights (low beam) shall be turned on at their respective times after the lights reach the lighting illuminance level from the off state and after the lighting delay time T1 (approximately 0.8 seconds or 30 m driving, whichever is established first).
After the lights have reached the OFF level and the OFF delay time T2 (approx. 3.5 seconds) has elapsed, the position lights and headlights (low beam) are turned off at the respective times.
Rainfall mode control (vehicles equipped with auto light system)

When the system detects 10 consecutive ON/OFF operations of the front wiper auto stop (AS) switch while driving in auto light mode, it shifts to rainfall mode and changes the threshold for on/off illumination to turn the lights on earlier and off later during rainy weather.

items.text: When the ignition is set to the OFF (LOCK) mode
If the front wiper auto stop (AS) switch ON/OFF operation cannot be detected for more than 15 seconds

blocks.text: Warning function (vehicles equipped with auto light system)

In the event of an auto light sensor failure or if there is an error in the signal transmitted from the MICU via B-CAN communication, the auto light control unit/sensor sends an indicator signal to the combination meter.
Sensitivity adjustment function (vehicles equipped with auto light system)
The HDS and combination meters allow customization of the sensitivity adjustment function of the auto light system, with five levels (Min, Lo, Mid, Hi, Max) of on/off timing selectable.
